get.description: Root endpoint

List every capability advertised by a heartbeating worker.

Returns one entry per ``(agent_id, command)`` pair with its pool,
description, and the set of worker ids currently serving it. Used by
``MonetClient.list_capabilities`` so clients can discover
user-defined agents at runtime.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Agents Get

post.description:
Run a single ``agent_id:command`` invocation and return the result.

Wraps the orchestration-side ``invoke_agent`` primitive so clients
can drive a single capability without composing a graph. Fails with
400 if the capability isn't in the manifest.

schema.title: Response Invoke Agent Endpoint Api V1 Agents  Agent Id   Command  Invoke Post

get.description:
List recent artifacts for a thread, newest-first.

``thread_id`` is required â€” listing across all threads is not supported
in keyless dev mode to avoid cross-tenant data exposure.

``cursor`` is an opaque ISO 8601 timestamp from ``next_cursor`` of the
previous response. Pass it to fetch the next page.

parameters.description: Required: filter to this thread
parameters.name: thread_id

schema.description: Required: filter to this thread
schema.title: Thread Id

parameters.description: ISO 8601 created_at cursor
parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: ISO 8601 created_at cursor
schema.title: Cursor

get.description:
Stream the raw bytes of an artifact by id.

Lets a chat UI render a clickable deep link (``â€¦/api/v1/artifacts/<id>``)
so the operator can open a plan's full work brief in the browser.
Returns the artifact content with its recorded ``content_type`` and
a ``Content-Disposition`` hinting at the artifact key.

Render an artifact as a human-readable HTML page.

Work-brief JSON artifacts (``{goal, nodes, assumptions?}``) are
rendered as a typed, dependency-annotated page; anything else falls
back to pretty-printed JSON or plain text. This is the endpoint the
chat TUI deep-links to so users get a readable view instead of raw
bytes.
List active deployments, optionally filtered by pool.

schema.title: Response List Deployments Api V1 Deployments Get

get.description:
Health check. No authentication required.

On a Redis-backed queue, PING is required to return 200 â€” a Redis
outage must surface as 503 here so load balancers stop routing to
broken replicas instead of returning a false-healthy 200.

Additive fields ``version``, ``queue_backend``, and ``uptime_seconds``
are present unconditionally so Go clients can perform version-compat
checks at startup without a separate request.

post.description:
Claim one task from the pool, server-blocking up to ``block_ms``.

The server issues ``XREADGROUP ... BLOCK block_ms`` (or the memory
equivalent) so the worker's HTTP request waits until a task lands
or the timeout elapses. Returns the task record on success or 204
No Content on timeout.

``body.consumer_id`` must be a ``worker_id`` that is currently
heartbeating for *pool*; otherwise the claim is rejected with 403
(fixes cross-pool task poaching in S3 / S5 fleets).

schema.title: Response Claim From Pool Api V1 Pools  Pool  Claim Post

post.description: Post a successful result for a claimed task.

schema.title: Response Complete Task Api V1 Tasks  Task Id  Complete Post

post.description: Post a failure for a claimed task.

schema.title: Response Fail Task Api V1 Tasks  Task Id  Fail Post

post.description:
Fire-and-forget progress event from a remote worker.

Rejects bodies larger than ``MAX_INLINE_PAYLOAD_BYTES`` (413). The
server publishes to Redis Pub/Sub (or the in-memory fan-out); lost
publishes are acceptable per ADR Â§progress-flow loss budget.

schema.title: Response Post Progress Api V1 Tasks  Task Id  Progress Post

post.description:
Unified registration + liveness ping for a worker.

First call from an unknown ``worker_id`` registers; subsequent calls
reconcile the capability set. The :class:`CapabilityIndex` is the
authoritative view; the deployment store tracks liveness for the
stale sweeper.

schema.title: Response Worker Heartbeat Api V1 Workers  Worker Id  Heartbeat Post

get.description:
List all assistants owned by the authenticated user.

Returns every assistant without filtering. Use the search endpoint for
filtered queries.

post.description:
Create a new assistant.

An assistant is a configured instance of a graph. Provide a `graph_id`
referencing a graph defined in your `aegra.json`. If `assistant_id` is
omitted, one is auto-generated. Set `if_exists` to `"do_nothing"` for
idempotent creation.

Count assistants matching the given filters.

Accepts the same filter parameters as the search endpoint but returns only
the total count.

schema.title: Response Count Assistants Assistants Count Post

post.description:
Search assistants with filters.

Filter by name, description, graph ID, or metadata. Results are paginated
via `limit` and `offset`.

schema.title: Response Search Assistants Assistants Search Post

delete.description:
Delete an assistant by its ID.

Permanently removes the assistant and all of its versions. This action
cannot be undone.

get.description:
Get an assistant by its ID.

Returns the latest version of the assistant. Returns 404 if the assistant
does not exist or does not belong to the authenticated user.

patch.description:
Update an assistant by its ID.

Partial update: only fields included in the request body are changed.
Creates a new version of the assistant.

get.description:
Get the graph structure for visualization.

Returns a JSON representation of the graph's nodes and edges suitable for
rendering in graph visualizers. Use `xray` to expand subgraph nodes into
their internal structure.

parameters.description: Expand subgraph nodes. Pass true or a depth integer to control nesting.
parameters.name: xray

schema.description: Expand subgraph nodes. Pass true or a depth integer to control nesting.
schema.title: Xray

post.description:
Pin a specific version as the latest version of an assistant.

After calling this endpoint, the assistant will use the specified version's
configuration when executing runs.

get.description:
Get the JSON schemas for an assistant's graph.

Returns the input, output, state, and config schemas derived from the
underlying graph's type annotations.

Get subgraphs of an assistant.

Returns the subgraph definitions used by this assistant's graph. Set
`recurse=true` to include deeply nested subgraphs, or filter to a single
namespace.

parameters.description: Recursively include nested subgraphs.
parameters.name: recurse

schema.description: Recursively include nested subgraphs.
schema.title: Recurse

parameters.description: Filter to a specific subgraph namespace.
parameters.name: namespace

schema.description: Filter to a specific subgraph namespace.
schema.title: Namespace

post.description:
List all versions of an assistant.

Returns versions ordered from newest to oldest. Each version captures the
assistant's configuration at the time of creation or update.

schema.title: Response List Assistant Versions Assistants  Assistant Id  Versions Post

get.description:
Check the health of all server components.

Verifies connectivity to PostgreSQL, the checkpoint backend, and the
store backend. Returns 503 if any component is unhealthy.

Get service information.

Returns the server name, version, and feature flags. This endpoint does
not require authentication.

Kubernetes liveness probe.

Always returns 200 to indicate the process is alive. Does not check
backend connectivity.

Kubernetes readiness probe.

Returns 200 when the server can accept traffic (database and graph
backends are initialized). Returns 503 otherwise.

post.description:
Create a stateless background run.

Generates an ephemeral thread, delegates to the threaded ``create_run``
endpoint, and schedules cleanup as a background task (unless
``on_completion="keep"``).

Create a stateless run and stream its execution.

Generates an ephemeral thread, delegates to the threaded
``create_and_stream_run`` endpoint, and deletes the thread after the
stream finishes (unless ``on_completion="keep"``).

Create a stateless run and wait for completion.

Generates an ephemeral thread, delegates to the threaded ``wait_for_run``
endpoint, and deletes the thread after the response finishes streaming
(unless ``on_completion="keep"``).

delete.description:
Delete an item from the store.

Accepts parameters via JSON body (`namespace` + `key`) or query
parameters. The JSON body takes precedence when both are provided.

parameters.description: Key of the item to delete (query param alternative).
parameters.name: key

schema.title: Key

schema.description: Namespace path (query param alternative).
schema.title: Namespace

get.description:
Get an item from the store by key.

Returns 404 if no item exists at the given namespace and key.

parameters.description: Key of the item to retrieve.
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Key of the item to retrieve.
schema.title: Key

parameters.description: Namespace path. Use dot-separated string or repeated query params.
parameters.name: namespace

schema.description: Namespace path. Use dot-separated string or repeated query params.
schema.title: Namespace

put.description:
Create or update an item in the store.

If an item with the same namespace and key already exists, its value is
overwritten. Values must be JSON objects (dictionaries).

post.description:
Search items in the store.

Filter items by namespace prefix, key-value metadata filters, or semantic
query. Results are paginated via `limit` and `offset`.

List namespaces in the store.

Returns the namespace paths that contain items. Filter by prefix, suffix,
or maximum depth.

get.description:
List all threads owned by the authenticated user.

Returns every thread without filtering. Use the search endpoint for
filtered queries.

post.description:
Create a new conversation thread.

Threads hold conversation state and checkpoint history. Provide a
`thread_id` for idempotent creation, or let the server generate one.
Set `if_exists` to `"do_nothing"` to return the existing thread when the
ID already exists instead of raising a 409 conflict.

Search threads with filters.

Filter by status or metadata key-value pairs. Results are paginated via
`limit` and `offset` and ordered by creation time (newest first).

schema.title: Response Search Threads Threads Search Post

delete.description:
Delete a thread by its ID.

Permanently removes the thread and its metadata. Any active runs on the
thread are automatically cancelled before deletion. Checkpoint history
stored in the graph backend is not affected.

schema.title: Response Delete Thread Threads  Thread Id  Delete

get.description:
Get a thread by its ID.

Returns 404 if the thread does not exist or does not belong to the
authenticated user.

patch.description:
Update a thread's metadata.

Merges the provided metadata with the existing metadata (shallow merge).

get.description:
Get the checkpoint history for a thread.

Returns a list of past states ordered from newest to oldest. Use `limit`
to control how many states are returned and `before` to paginate.

parameters.description: Number of states to return
parameters.name: limit

schema.title: Limit

parameters.name: before

schema.title: Before

parameters.name: subgraphs

schema.title: Subgraphs

parameters.name: checkpoint_ns

schema.description: Checkpoint namespace
schema.title: Checkpoint Ns

parameters.description: JSON-encoded metadata filter
parameters.name: metadata

schema.description: JSON-encoded metadata filter
schema.title: Metadata
Response Get Thread History Get Threads  Thread Id  History Get

post.description:
Get the checkpoint history for a thread (POST variant).

Returns a list of past states ordered from newest to oldest. Use `limit`
to control how many states are returned and `before` to paginate.

schema.title: Response Get Thread History Post Threads  Thread Id  History Post

List runs for a thread.

Returns runs ordered by creation time (newest first). Use `status` to
filter and `limit`/`offset` to paginate.

parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return
parameters.name: limit

schema.title: Offset

parameters.name: status

Response List Runs Threads  Thread Id  Runs Get

post.description:
Create and execute a new run.

Starts graph execution asynchronously and returns the run record
immediately with status `pending`. Poll the run or use the stream
endpoint to follow progress. Provide either `input` or `command` (for
human-in-the-loop resumption) but not both.

Create a new run and stream its execution via SSE.

Returns a `text/event-stream` response with Server-Sent Events. Each
event has a `type` field (e.g. `values`, `updates`, `messages`,
`metadata`, `end`) and a JSON `data` payload.

Set `on_disconnect` to `"continue"` if the run should keep executing
after the client disconnects (default is `"cancel"`). Use `stream_mode`
to control which event types are emitted.

Create a run, execute it, and wait for completion.

Returns a chunked ``application/json`` response with periodic ``\n``
heartbeat bytes to keep the connection alive. The final chunk is the
JSON result. Uses ``BG_JOB_TIMEOUT_SECS`` (default 1 hour) as the
safety-net timeout.

Sessions are managed manually (not via ``Depends``) to avoid holding a
pool connection during the long wait.

delete.description:
Delete a run record.

If the run is active (pending or running) and `force=0`, returns 409
Conflict. Set `force=1` to cancel the run first (best-effort) and then
delete it. Returns 204 No Content on success.

parameters.description: Set to 1 to cancel an active run before deleting it.
parameters.name: force

schema.description: Set to 1 to cancel an active run before deleting it.
schema.title: Force

get.description:
Get a run by its ID.

Returns the current state of the run including its status, input, output,
and error information.

patch.description:
Update a run's status.

Primarily used to interrupt a running execution. Set `status` to
`"interrupted"` to cooperatively stop the run.

post.description:
Cancel or interrupt a running execution.

Use `action=cancel` to hard-cancel the run immediately, or
`action=interrupt` to cooperatively interrupt (the graph can handle the
interrupt and save partial state). Set `wait=1` to block until the
background task has fully settled before returning the updated run.

parameters.description: Set to 1 to wait for the run task to settle before returning.
parameters.name: wait

schema.description: Set to 1 to wait for the run task to settle before returning.
schema.title: Wait

parameters.description: Cancellation strategy: 'cancel' for hard cancel, 'interrupt' for cooperative interrupt.
parameters.name: action

schema.description: Cancellation strategy: 'cancel' for hard cancel, 'interrupt' for cooperative interrupt.
schema.title: Action

get.description:
Wait for a run to complete and return its output.

Returns a chunked ``application/json`` response. While the run is still
executing, the server sends periodic ``\n`` heartbeat bytes to keep the
connection alive through proxies and load balancers (AWS ALB, Cloudflare,
etc.). The final chunk is the JSON result. Leading whitespace is ignored
by JSON parsers, so clients can parse the concatenated body normally.

If the run is already in a terminal state, the output is returned
immediately with no heartbeat overhead.

Sessions are managed manually (not via ``Depends``) to avoid holding a
pool connection during the long wait.

Stream an existing run's execution via SSE.

Attach to a run that was created without streaming (e.g. via the create
endpoint) to receive its events in real time. If the run has already
finished, a single `end` event is emitted. Use the `Last-Event-ID`
header to resume from a specific event after a disconnect.

parameters.description: Override the stream mode for this connection.
parameters.name: _stream_mode

schema.description: Override the stream mode for this connection.
schema.title: Stream Mode

get.description:
Get the current state of a thread.

Returns the latest checkpoint's values, pending next nodes, interrupt
data, and metadata. If the thread has no associated graph yet (no runs
executed), returns an empty state.

parameters.description: Include states from subgraphs
parameters.name: subgraphs

schema.description: Include states from subgraphs
schema.title: Subgraphs

parameters.description: Checkpoint namespace to scope lookup
parameters.name: checkpoint_ns

schema.description: Checkpoint namespace to scope lookup
schema.title: Checkpoint Ns

post.description:
Update thread state or retrieve it via POST.

When `values` is provided, creates a new checkpoint with the updated state.
Use `as_node` to attribute the update to a specific graph node. When
`values` is null, this endpoint acts as a POST-based alternative to the
GET state endpoint (useful when passing complex checkpoint/subgraph
parameters in the request body).

schema.title: Response Update Thread State Threads  Thread Id  State Post

post.description:
Get the thread state at a specific checkpoint (POST variant).

Identical to the GET checkpoint endpoint but accepts the checkpoint
configuration in the request body. Useful when the checkpoint namespace
contains characters that are awkward in URL paths.
post.summary: Get Thread State At Checkpoint Post

get.description:
Get the thread state at a specific checkpoint.

Use this to inspect historical state at any point in the thread's
execution history. Returns 404 if the checkpoint does not exist.

parameters.description: Include states from subgraphs
parameters.name: subgraphs

schema.description: Include states from subgraphs
schema.title: Subgraphs

parameters.description: Checkpoint namespace to scope lookup
parameters.name: checkpoint_ns

schema.description: Checkpoint namespace to scope lookup
schema.title: Checkpoint Ns
